summarization.a: Chart plots loss tangent against applied voltage for BST/MgO, BST/TiO2/Si and BST/Ta2O5/Si samples.

a.question: How does the loss tangent vary with applied voltage?
a.answer: The loss tangent increases with increase in applied voltage.

a.question: How does the loss tangent of BST/TiO2/Si, and BST/Ta2O5/Si compare
a.answer: After an applied voltage of 2 V, increasing the voltage the loss tangent of  BST/Ta2O5/Si is double that of BST/TiO2/Si

a.question: Which films show the lowest loss tangent?
a.answer: BST films grown on MgO showed  the lowest loss tangent

a.question: Why is the loss tangent of BST high for the other substrates? And why is the loss tangent important?
a.answer:
The loss tangent of BST grown on TiO2 /Si and Ta2O5 /Si is high, presumably due to the lossy Si substrate. The figure of merit (FOM), ratio of
tunability to dielectric loss, is very important for microwave tunable device applications. At an applied voltage of 5 V, the
FOM of BST/TiO2 and BST/Ta2O5 were 5.22 and 1.57, respectively. The BST films grown on TiO2 and Ta2O5 buffered
Si substrates showed relatively lower FOM values as compared to the FOM (11.87) of BST/MgO due to the lossy Si substrate in the former case
